title: When The Rose Is Faded
content:
When the rose is faded,
 Memory may still dwell on
 Her beauty shadowed,
 And the sweet smell gone.

 That vanishing loveliness,
 That burdening breath
 No bond of life hath then
 Nor grief of death.

 'Tis the immortal thought
 Whose passion still
 Makes of the changing
 The unchangeable.

 Oh, thus thy beauty,
 Loveliest on earth to me,
 Dark with no sorrow, shines
 And burns, with Thee.
meaning:
Walter De La Mare's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "When The Rose Is Faded"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
